data.title: record-review.sh: finding file overlap check rejects valid merge-commit reviews -> orchestrator must use empty findings array workaround
data.description:
## Expected Behavior
record-review.sh should accept reviewer findings for merge commits where the findings reference files from the pre-merge diff (the actual reviewed content), even though the merge commit's diff includes additional files from the incoming branch.

## Actual Behavior
record-review.sh validates that finding file fields overlap with changed files in the current git diff. For merge commits, the diff includes files brought in by the merged branch that weren't part of the original review. The overlap check fails with: 'reviewer findings files do not overlap with any changed files in the diff'. The only workaround is to submit an empty findings array, which loses the actual review feedback.

## Reproduction
1. Review changes on session branch (findings reference e.g. tests/scripts/test-foo.sh)
2. Merge a worktree branch that adds plugins/dso/agents/bar.md
3. Merge commit diff includes both test-foo.sh and bar.md
4. record-review.sh rejects because findings only reference test-foo.sh but the merge diff has changed to include bar.md as well
5. Only way to unblock: write findings with empty findings array

## Impact
Review feedback is lost on merge commits. The review gate becomes a formality (empty findings always pass) rather than carrying forward actual reviewer analysis.
